dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9161 IH: Secure Aluminum Supply Chains Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Stevens introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs

form.official-title: To require the United States International Trade Commission to investigate national security concerns regarding the exportation of aluminum scrap to countries of concern, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Secure Aluminum Supply Chains Act.

section.header: Investigation of national and economic security risks

subsection.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the United States International Trade Commission shall submit to Congress a report that includes—

paragraph.text: the results of a review of the national and economic security risks posed by—

subparagraph.text: United States exports of aluminum scrap to countries of concern; and

United States aluminum scrap otherwise available to entities organized under the laws of or otherwise subject to the control of a country of concern (including subsidiaries of such entities), whether or not exported directly to the countries of concern; and

paragraph.text: recommendations on how Congress and the President can address any national or economic security risks identified in the report.

subsection.text: In conducting the review described in subsection (a)(1), the Commission shall consult as appropriate with stakeholders in the aluminum scrap and export industries, such as—

paragraph.text: private entities engaged in aluminum production in the United States;
private entities in the metals recycling industry; and
labor organizations involved in aluminum production in the United States.

subsection.text: For purposes of the report required by subsection (a)—

paragraph.text: the term aluminum scrap means recycled or discarded materials primarily containing or derived from aluminum; and
the term country of concern means the Russian Federation, the People's Republic of China, the Islamic Republic of Iran, and the Democratic People's Republic of Korea.
